The DDR-immune fitness score: a biomarker for guiding parp and immunotherapy synergy in extensive-stage small cell lung cancer

evidence_entities.text: Despite the integration of PD-L1 inhibitors with chemotherapy, extensive-stage small-cell lung cancer (ES-SCLC) continues to portend a dismal prognosis, with a 5-year survival rate below 10%
DDR-IF-high tumors were characterized by significantly reduced CD8+ T cell infiltration (2.3-fold fewer, p = 0.004)
superior pooled objective response rate to PARP-ICB combinations (42% vs 18%; risk ratio 2.3, 95% CI 1.3-4.2; p = 0.003)

notes: The DDR-IF score is described as an exploratory biomarker requiring prospective validation
The study integrates data from 82 SCLC tumors and six phase II trials
Statistical significance reported for CD8+ T cell infiltration (p=0.004) and objective response rate (p=0.003)
